description: Instant Conversations — Eliminate LLM call at session start by sending pre-known opening text directly to TTS, reducing time-to-first-audio from 3-8s to <1s for all core sessions

userStories.title: Database migration for pre-computed opening text columns
userStories.description: As a developer, I need storage columns on user_progress for pre-computed L2/L3 opening text so the system can store and retrieve them at session start.
acceptanceCriteria: Create migration file at supabase/migrations/20260220_instant_conversations.sql
Migration adds precomputed_opening_text TEXT column (nullable) to public.user_progress using ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS
Migration adds precomputed_opening_at TIMESTAMPTZ column (nullable) to public.user_progress using ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS
Migration includes COMMENT ON COLUMN for both columns explaining their purpose
In types/database.types.ts: user_progress.Row has precomputed_opening_text: string | null and precomputed_opening_at: string | null (alphabetically sorted among existing fields)
In types/database.types.ts: user_progress.Insert has precomputed_opening_text?: string | null and precomputed_opening_at?: string | null
In types/database.types.ts: user_progress.Update has precomputed_opening_text?: string | null and precomputed_opening_at?: string | null
Existing user_progress rows are not affected (both columns default to NULL)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Follow the pattern from supabase/migrations/20260216_email_unsubscribe.sql (simple ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS). TypeScript types in types/database.types.ts must be updated manually since npm run db:types requires a live Supabase connection. The user_progress type is at lines 656-723 in types/database.types.ts — search for 'user_progress' to find the Row, Insert, Update sections. Fields are alphabetically sorted within each section. The email_unsubscribed_at column (added in a previous Ralph run) is a good pattern to follow for placement.

userStories.title: Opening text endpoint (GET /api/session/opening-text)
userStories.description: As the client application, I want to fetch the opening text for a core session so I can determine whether the fast-start path is available.
acceptanceCriteria: Create server/api/session/opening-text.get.ts
Endpoint uses serverSupabaseUser() for authentication (returns 401 if unauthenticated)
Given sessionType !== 'core', returns { text: null, source: null }
Given illusionLayer === 'intellectual' and valid illusionKey, returns { text: ILLUSION_OPENING_MESSAGES[illusionKey], source: 'static' }
Given illusionLayer === 'intellectual' and illusionKey not found in ILLUSION_OPENING_MESSAGES, returns { text: null, source: null }
Given illusionLayer === 'emotional' or 'identity' and precomputed_opening_text exists in user_progress for this user, returns { text: '<precomputed text>', source: 'precomputed' }
Given illusionLayer === 'emotional' or 'identity' and precomputed_opening_text is NULL, returns { text: null, source: null }
Returns 400 for missing required query params (illusionKey, illusionLayer, sessionType)
Logs with [instant-start] tag: 'Opening text resolved' or 'Opening text not available' with illusionKey, illusionLayer, source/reason
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: File: server/api/session/opening-text.get.ts. Import ILLUSION_OPENING_MESSAGES from server/utils/prompts (defined at lines 182-202 in server/utils/prompts/index.ts — it's a Record<IllusionKey, string> with keys: stress_relief, pleasure, willpower, focus, identity). Import IllusionKey, IllusionLayer, SessionType from server/utils/llm/task-types. Auth: serverSupabaseUser() from #supabase/server for auth check, serverSupabaseServiceRole() for DB read on user_progress. Read query params with getQuery(event). For L2/L3 path: query user_progress.precomputed_opening_text where user_id matches. The user_progress table has a UNIQUE(user_id) constraint so the read is already optimally indexed. Use console.log for [instant-start] tagged log messages.

userStories.title: Session bootstrap endpoint (POST /api/session/bootstrap)
userStories.description: As the client application, I want to create a conversation record with the opening message in a single call so the session is initialized while audio plays.
acceptanceCriteria: Create server/api/session/bootstrap.post.ts
Endpoint uses serverSupabaseUser() for authentication (returns 401 if unauthenticated)
Returns 400 for missing required body fields (illusionKey, illusionLayer, sessionType, openingText)
Creates conversation record in conversations table with fields: user_id, model (from getDefaultModel()), title (from ILLUSION_NAMES[illusionKey]), session_type, illusion_key, illusion_layer
Saves opening text as first message in messages table with: conversation_id, role='assistant', content=openingText, input_modality='text'

Cancels pending evidence bridge check-ins for this user/illusion: UPDATE check_in_schedule SET status='cancelled', cancellation_reason='user_continued_immediately' WHERE user_id=X AND trigger_illusion_key=illusionKey AND check_in_type='evidence_bridge' AND status IN ('scheduled', 'sent')
Evidence bridge cancellation is non-blocking (catch and log errors, never fail the endpoint)
Returns { conversationId: '<uuid>' } on success
Logs with [instant-start] tag: 'Bootstrap success' or 'Bootstrap failed' with conversationId/error
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: File: server/api/session/bootstrap.post.ts. Mirror the conversation creation from server/api/chat.post.ts lines 363-380: insert into conversations with user_id, model, title, session_type, illusion_key, illusion_layer, then .select('id').single(). Mirror evidence bridge cancellation from chat.post.ts lines 382-402. Auth: serverSupabaseUser() from #supabase/server for auth, serverSupabaseServiceRole() for DB writes (same as /api/chat pattern). Import ILLUSION_NAMES from server/utils/prompts (line 28 in index.ts — Record<IllusionKey, string>). Import getDefaultModel from server/utils/llm (line 22 in server/utils/llm/index.ts). Import IllusionKey, IllusionLayer from server/utils/llm/task-types. Read body with readBody(event). The check_in_schedule table has trigger_illusion_key column for evidence bridge check-ins.

userStories.title: Fast-start path in useVoiceChat.startConversation()
userStories.description: As a user starting a core session, I want the coach's voice to begin within ~1 second so that the session feels immediate and conversational.
acceptanceCriteria: In composables/useVoiceChat.ts, add a fast-start branch at the TOP of startConversation() (before the existing enableStreamingTTS check at line 401)
Fast-start branch only executes when sessionType === 'core' && illusionKey is truthy
Fast-start branch calls GET /api/session/opening-text with query params { illusionKey, illusionLayer, sessionType }
If opening text is available (result.text is truthy): call voiceSession.playAIResponse(text) and bootstrapWithRetry() in parallel (bootstrap does NOT block audio)
After playAIResponse succeeds: push { role: 'assistant', content: text } to messages.value array
After bootstrap resolves: if result has conversationId, set conversationId.value = result.conversationId
If opening text is null OR playAIResponse fails: fall through to existing code (no early return, no error — the existing streaming/non-streaming logic handles it)
Add bootstrapWithRetry() private function: calls POST /api/session/bootstrap, retries up to 3 times with exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 4s), returns { conversationId } or null after max 15s window
Client-side console.log with [useVoiceChat] tag when fast-start path is used or when falling back (with reason)
Non-core sessions (check_in, ceremony, reinforcement) skip fast-start entirely — existing flow unchanged
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: File: composables/useVoiceChat.ts. The startConversation() function is at line 396. Insert the fast-start branch INSIDE the try block (after error.value = null and isLoading.value = true, before the existing if (enableStreamingTTS) at line 401). Key variables already available as closure: illusionKey (line 61), illusionLayer (line 62, default 'intellectual'), sessionType (line 63, default 'core'), messages (ref line 80), conversationId (ref line 81), isLoading (ref line 82), voiceSession (line 90). voiceSession.playAIResponse(text) returns a Promise — it internally calls /api/voice/synthesize, plays audio, and handles word-by-word highlighting. Use $fetch for API calls (auto-available in Nuxt composables). The fallback is simply: if the fast-start branch doesn't return true, the existing code below it runs. The bootstrapWithRetry function should be defined above startConversation within the useVoiceChat composable scope. If bootstrap fails all retries, conversationId stays null — sendMessage() already handles null conversationId by falling through to /api/chat which creates the conversation (lines 418-433 for non-streaming, lines 126-146 for streaming).

userStories.title: Pre-computation background job for L2/L3 opening text
userStories.description: As the system, I want to pre-compute the L2/L3 opening message after a session completes so that the next session can use the fast-start path.
acceptanceCriteria: Create server/utils/session/precompute-opening.ts with exported async function precomputeOpeningText(params)
Function accepts params: { supabase: SupabaseClient, userId: string, illusionKey: IllusionKey, nextLayer: IllusionLayer }
Function fetches user intake for userContext (query user_intake for product_types, usage_frequency, years_using, previous_attempts, triggers)
Function builds session context using buildSessionContext(supabase, userId, illusionKey, 'core') and formatContextForPrompt()
Function builds cross-layer context using buildCrossLayerContext(supabase, userId, illusionKey, nextLayer) and formatCrossLayerContext()
Function builds bridge context using buildBridgeContext(crossLayerCtx)
Function builds system prompt using buildSystemPrompt({ illusionKey, userContext, isNewConversation: true, personalizationContext, bridgeContext, illusionLayer: nextLayer })
Function calls getModelRouter().chat() with system prompt + generation prompt asking for opening message only (2-4 sentences, warm, acknowledges progress, ends with open question)
On success: stores generated text in user_progress.precomputed_opening_text and precomputed_opening_at
On first failure: retries once after 500ms delay
On both failures: logs errors with [precompute-opening] tag and exits (no text stored, fallback at session start)
Empty LLM response treated as failure (triggers retry)
Logs with [precompute-opening] tag on success (userId, illusionKey, nextLayer, textLength, attempt) and failure (error details)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: File: server/utils/session/precompute-opening.ts (new file). Import buildSessionContext, formatContextForPrompt from server/utils/personalization/context-builder (buildSessionContext at line 167, formatContextForPrompt at line 295). Import buildCrossLayerContext, formatCrossLayerContext from server/utils/personalization/cross-layer-context (buildCrossLayerContext at line 83, formatCrossLayerContext at line 135). Import buildBridgeContext from server/utils/session/bridge (line 12). Import buildSystemPrompt from server/utils/prompts (index.ts). Import getModelRouter, getDefaultModel from server/utils/llm (index.ts lines 7 and 22). Import IllusionKey, IllusionLayer from server/utils/llm/task-types. The generation prompt should instruct the LLM to output ONLY the opening message text (no preamble, no labels). The retry loop runs at most 2 iterations (attempt 0 and 1), with 500ms delay before the retry. Use the same UserContext interface that /api/chat uses for intake data. The SupabaseClient type is from @supabase/supabase-js.

userStories.title: Trigger pre-computation on L1/L2 session completion
userStories.description: As the system, I want to trigger the pre-computation job when an L1 or L2 session completes so that the opening text is ready for the user's next session.
acceptanceCriteria: In server/api/progress/complete-session.post.ts, add a fire-and-forget call to precomputeOpeningText() after L1 or L2 completion
When illusionLayer === 'intellectual': call with nextLayer: 'emotional'
When illusionLayer === 'emotional': call with nextLayer: 'identity'
When illusionLayer === 'identity': do NOT call precomputeOpeningText (next session is a different illusion's L1 which uses static text)
Pre-computation failure does NOT affect the session completion response (fire-and-forget with .catch() error logging)
Import precomputeOpeningText from server/utils/session/precompute-opening
Log with [complete-session] tag when pre-computation is triggered or fails
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: File: server/api/progress/complete-session.post.ts. The layer progression block is at lines 115-337. The fire-and-forget pattern is already established by scheduleCheckIns() (lines 238-252) and scheduleEvidenceBridgeCheckIn() (lines 312-326) — follow the same .then().catch() non-blocking pattern. Add the pre-computation call near the evidence bridge scheduling (lines 312-326) since both are triggered after L1/L2 completion. Available variables: userId from user.sub, effectiveIllusionKey, illusionLayer (from the request body). The supabase client is already available in the handler. Only trigger within the layer progression block (when illusionLayer is provided), NOT in the legacy block (lines 413-428).
